etymology.description: two bundles 東(東) (dōng, 'east') -> pair or peer, same kind -> 囗(囗) (wéi, 'enclosure') added for distinction, later corrupted to 甘 -> bronze inscription -> 曰(曰) (yuē, 'speak') in Seal Script -> 𣍘 -> modern 曹(曹) (cáo, 'ministry; a surname')
etymology.interpretation: 𣍘 is an intermediate form leading to 曹(曹) (cáo, 'ministry; a surname'), developed from the concept of pairing bundles, evolving through the addition and eventual transformation of elements like 囗(囗), 甘, and 曰(曰). This character demonstrates the evolution of form through historical scripts, creating a distinct and recognizable modern entity.
